instruction: My maize has these symptoms: Small holes in leaves, sawdust-like frass, caterpillars inside whorl. What is wrong?
output: This sounds like fall armyworm. Apply Bt (Bacillus thuringiensis) spray or neem-based pesticide. For severe cases, use emamectin benzoate. Scout fields early morning when larvae are active. To prevent it in the future: Early planting, intercropping with beans or cowpeas, use push-pull technology with Desmodium and Napier grass

instruction: My tomato has these symptoms: Water-soaked lesions on leaves turning brown/black, white mold on undersides in humid weather, fruit rot. What is wrong?
output: This sounds like late blight. Apply mancozeb or copper-based fungicide every 7 days. Remove and burn affected leaves. Ensure good air circulation. To prevent it in the future: Use resistant varieties, avoid overhead irrigation, stake plants, apply preventive copper spray

instruction: How do I treat late blight?

instruction: How do I prevent late blight?
output: Use resistant varieties, avoid overhead irrigation, stake plants, apply preventive copper spray

instruction: My tomato has these symptoms: Rapid wilting of entire plant, brown discoloration of stem vascular tissue, sticky bacterial ooze when stem is cut. What is wrong?
output: This sounds like bacterial wilt. No chemical cure. Remove and burn infected plants. Solarize soil. Do not replant same crop for 3+ seasons. To prevent it in the future: Use certified disease-free seeds, rotate crops, improve drainage, use resistant varieties

instruction: My maize has these symptoms: Long elliptical gray-green to tan lesions on leaves, starting from lower leaves. What is wrong?
output: This sounds like northern leaf blight. Apply mancozeb or azoxystrobin fungicide. Remove heavily infected lower leaves. To prevent it in the future: Plant resistant hybrids, rotate crops, plow under crop residue

instruction: How do I treat northern leaf blight?
output: Apply mancozeb or azoxystrobin fungicide. Remove heavily infected lower leaves.

instruction: How do I prevent northern leaf blight?
output: Plant resistant hybrids, rotate crops, plow under crop residue

instruction: My wheat has these symptoms: Small orange-brown pustules on leaves and stems, powdery spores when touched. What is wrong?
output: This sounds like rust. Apply propiconazole or tebuconazole fungicide at first sign. Repeat after 14 days if needed. To prevent it in the future: Plant resistant varieties, avoid dense planting, remove volunteer plants

instruction: My cabbage has these symptoms: White powdery coating on leaves, stunted growth, leaf curling. What is wrong?
output: This sounds like powdery mildew. Apply sulfur-based fungicide or potassium bicarbonate spray. Neem oil also effective. To prevent it in the future: Good spacing, avoid overhead watering, plant in full sun

instruction: My cabbage has these symptoms: Clusters of small green/black insects on undersides of leaves, sticky honeydew, curling leaves, sooty mold. What is wrong?
output: This sounds like aphid infestation. Spray with neem oil or insecticidal soap. Introduce ladybugs. For severe cases, use imidacloprid. To prevent it in the future: Companion planting with marigolds, garlic, or chives. Remove weeds. Use reflective mulch.

instruction: I have clay soil. How should I manage it?
output: Improve drainage with organic matter. Add gypsum to break up heavy clay. Avoid working when wet. Good for rice and wheat.

instruction: What crops grow well in clay soil?
output: Improve drainage with organic matter. Add gypsum to break up heavy clay. Avoid working when wet. Good for rice and wheat.

instruction: I have loam soil. How should I manage it?
output: Ideal soil. Maintain with regular compost. Good for most crops. Apply mulch to retain moisture.

instruction: What crops grow well in loam soil?
output: Ideal soil. Maintain with regular compost. Good for most crops. Apply mulch to retain moisture.

instruction: I have sandy soil. How should I manage it?
output: Add compost and manure to improve water retention. Irrigate frequently in small amounts. Good for root crops like carrots and onions.

instruction: What crops grow well in sandy soil?
output: Add compost and manure to improve water retention. Irrigate frequently in small amounts. Good for root crops like carrots and onions.

instruction: I have laterite soil. How should I manage it?
output: Often low in nutrients. Add lime to correct pH. Heavy compost needed. Good for cashew and mango trees.

instruction: What crops grow well in laterite soil?
output: Often low in nutrients. Add lime to correct pH. Heavy compost needed. Good for cashew and mango trees.

instruction: I have black_cotton soil. How should I manage it?
output: Very fertile but cracks when dry. Add mulch. Excellent for cotton, sorghum, and sunflower.

instruction: What crops grow well in black_cotton soil?

instruction: When should I plant maize?
output: The best months to plant maize are March, April, October. It takes about 120 days to harvest. Plant with 75x25 spacing. Fertilizer: DAP at planting (50kg/acre), CAN at knee-height (50kg/acre).

instruction: What are good varieties of maize to plant?
output: Good varieties for maize include: Pioneer 3253, DK 8031, H614D, KH600-15A. Choose based on your local conditions and market demand.

instruction: What pests attack maize?
output: Common pests for maize include: fall armyworm, stem borer, aphids. Scout your fields twice weekly, checking under leaves and inside whorls. Early detection saves 80% of treatment cost.

instruction: When should I plant beans?
output: The best months to plant beans are March, April, October, November. It takes about 75 days to harvest. Plant with 40x15 spacing. Fertilizer: DAP at planting (25kg/acre), no nitrogen needed (nitrogen fixer).

instruction: What are good varieties of beans to plant?
output: Good varieties for beans include: KAT B1, KAT B9, Rose Coco, Canadian Wonder. Choose based on your local conditions and market demand.

instruction: What pests attack beans?
output: Common pests for beans include: aphids, bean fly, pod borer. Scout your fields twice weekly, checking under leaves and inside whorls. Early detection saves 80% of treatment cost.

instruction: When should I plant tomato?
output: The best months to plant tomato are March, September, October. It takes about 90 days to harvest. Plant with 60x45 spacing. Fertilizer: DAP at transplanting, CAN every 2 weeks, calcium spray weekly.

instruction: What are good varieties of tomato to plant?
output: Good varieties for tomato include: Rio Grande, Money Maker, Cal J, Kilele F1. Choose based on your local conditions and market demand.

instruction: What pests attack tomato?
output: Common pests for tomato include: tomato leafminer, whitefly, aphids. Scout your fields twice weekly, checking under leaves and inside whorls. Early detection saves 80% of treatment cost.

instruction: When should I plant onion?

output: Good varieties for rice include: Basmati 370, BW 196, IR 2793. Choose based on your local conditions and market demand.

instruction: What pests attack rice?

instruction: When should I plant wheat?
output: The best months to plant wheat are June, July. It takes about 110 days to harvest. Plant with broadcast_20cm_rows spacing. Fertilizer: DAP at planting (50kg/acre), CAN at tillering.

instruction: What are good varieties of wheat to plant?
output: Good varieties for wheat include: Kenya Fahari, Njoro BW2, Eagle 10. Choose based on your local conditions and market demand.

instruction: What pests attack wheat?
output: Common pests for wheat include: aphids, hessian fly. Scout your fields twice weekly, checking under leaves and inside whorls. Early detection saves 80% of treatment cost.

instruction: When should I plant sorghum?

instruction: What pests attack sorghum?

instruction: When should I plant cotton?

instruction: When should I plant sugarcane?

instruction: What pests attack cassava?

instruction: A trader is offering me 40 KES per kilo for tomatoes. Is that fair?
output: The current market rate for tomatoes is typically 60-100 KES per kilo depending on the season. 40 KES sounds low. Tell the trader you know the market rate and ask for at least 60 KES per kilo. If they refuse, check the next market day or try selling directly.

instruction: When is the best time to sell my maize?
output: Maize prices are usually lowest right after harvest when everyone is selling. If you can store your maize properly (dry to 13% moisture, use hermetic bags), wait 2-3 months after harvest when prices typically rise 20-40%. Market prices peak just before the next planting season.

instruction: How do I store my harvest to get better prices?
output: Dry your grain to 13% moisture content (bite a kernel — it should crack, not dent). Use hermetic storage bags (like PICS bags) that kill pests without chemicals. Store in a cool, dry place off the ground. Check monthly for any signs of damage. Properly stored maize can keep 6+ months.
